reasoning: The Lean statement faithfully captures the informal claim. The hypothesis `DifferentiableOn ℂ f D` with `D = ball 0 1` correctly encodes "holomorphic on the open unit disc"; `ContinuousOn f (closure D)` correctly encodes the continuous extension to the closed disc (and hence to the boundary ∂D); and `∀ z ∈ sphere 0 1, f z = 1/z` correctly encodes the boundary identity. The universal quantification over f : ℂ → ℂ combined with the negated conclusion expresses "no such f exists". Having f defined on all of ℂ rather than only on the closed disc is immaterial because the hypotheses and conclusion only reference values on closure D and on the unit sphere, so the statements are logically equivalent.
